observations: Opening line tightened from 'with the river damp coming off the bend' to 'with river damp off the bend' so the narration stays clipped and Sylvanas-shaped.
Sylvanas's first order now says 'not what you think it means,' which lands as a field command instead of abstract editorial language.
Lyriel's report changed from fragment stack to 'Same picture—life interrupted, no bodies, no blood, no sign of a fight,' preserving ranger brevity without flattening the cadence.
Jaina's granary diagnosis now runs 'Well water, root cellar floors, the trough by the smithy,' giving her speech a spoken list instead of report bullets.
Jaina's estimate shifted to 'Days. A week, maybe more.'—still uncertain, but built like speech rather than three metronomic fragments.
Closing line lost the editorial abstraction 'clean acceptance' and now stays inside Sylvanas's concrete read: Jaina has stopped waiting to be invited when the ground turns strange.
Opening paragraphs stay in Sylvanas's sensory lane: silence, animal reactions, distance, lane geometry, and household interruption rather than neutral village description.
The cellar scene keeps Miri as observed surface only—breathing, grip, stare, broken speech—without any narrator summary of her internal state.
The trader remains only a local grain man to Sylvanas on-page; the prose never lets later knowledge leak backward into suspicion.
Jaina's usefulness is rendered through what Sylvanas sees her do at the road cut and in the granary, not through narrator praise.
Millhaven still arrives as a direct callback from chapter2: first read from a ridge, then entered street-level, exactly as the chapter bridge required.
Jaina's contamination-reading competence is treated as established fact; chapter3 scales it up to village saturation instead of re-revealing the ability.
Neral remains the calm rear-guard and the obvious relay rider, then leaves north with the warning and the survivor exactly as the plan locked.
The unnamed escort ranger stays unnamed throughout and departs north with Neral, preserving the clean handoff to the four-rider southbound party.
The grain clue stays weak and unresolved: Miri remembers a newer grain man, the trader confirms another seller existed, and the chapter refuses to overcall that into proof.
Inserted participial connective tissue at the road kneel: 'The mage dropped to one knee in the road, pressing her free hand to the packed dirt...'
Shifted the spoor read to 'Too many feet had gone through ... , churning the earth to paste,' which breaks an early short-sentence chain.
Changed 'She stayed crouched...' to 'She stayed crouched ... , feeling cooling air and broken crust strip the sign away,' giving the beat movement and carry.

Added connective tissue through the cellar and dispatch runs: Lyriel stopping beside Sylvanas, Jaina kneeling beside Neral, Jaina following the markers, Neral turning to Miri, Neral pacing the horse north.
Removed continuous-action break markers so the chapter breathes in longer syntactic waves instead of resetting at hard visual seams.

continuity.crossChapter: Direct carry preserved from chapter2's Millhaven approach; the chapter opens inside the same approach pressure instead of resetting the village as a new destination.
observations: Removed both mid-chapter '###' breaks so the move from granary to spoor and from spoor to cellar stays causal rather than feeling like scene resets.
Removed the '## III' break before the dispatch so the proof at the road cut runs directly into the warning decision and Neral's handoff.
The spoor sequence still withholds dispatch until witness and ground agree, preserving Sylvanas's refusal to spend riders on uncertain direction.
Neral and the unnamed escort leave north with Miri, and the prose counts the southbound reduction cleanly to Sylvanas, Jaina, Cyndia, and Lyriel.
The closing relationship residue stays operational, not warm: Jaina takes position at Sylvanas's left because the work now demands it.
Abstract child-reaction line 'earned the child the edge of a look' became the more physical 'pulled the child's eyes off the horse.'
Trader beat 'He sat with that' now carries a visible action—still looking for the people who should have been there—instead of hanging on a summary sentence.
Open-country read at the road cut now names what the terrain means for moving bodies, replacing a flatter scenic filler sentence.
Fragment fatigue in Lyriel's report and Jaina's granary read was trimmed without blunting either woman's voice.
Visual seam markers were cut where they were functioning as throat-clearing rather than earned breaks.
Sylvanas's opening order now uses spoken pressure—'what you think it means'—instead of the colder, more document-like 'what it means.'
Neral's cellar talk stays plain-language throughout: dark, stink, water, horse, temper. No institutional varnish survived there.
Jaina's technical lines remain field-practical rather than clinical: taint, well water, root cellar floors, blacksmith's trough, layer, ground that answers.
Miri's testimony stays child-broken and sensory, never turning into a polished exposition block about plague mechanics.
The trader sounds like a road man talking markets and routes, not like a logistics brief in disguise.
